key_metrics: Gross margin compressed to 58.5% from 59.5% YoY
Diluted EPS decreased to $3.10 from $3.15 YoY
Inventories increased 19.4% to $1.72 billion compared to fiscal year-end
SG&A expenses increased 9.1% YoY to $951.7 million, outpacing revenue growth
qoq_changes: Implied sequential revenue growth from Q1 2025 ($2.37 billion) to Q2 2025 ($2.53 billion)
Cash and cash equivalents decreased to $1.16 billion from $1.98 billion at fiscal year-end
new_risks: Rising SG&A expenses and gross margin compression are pressuring overall profitability
Significant inventory build to $1.72 billion could elevate markdown risk if consumer demand softens
management_tone: Cautious; while top-line growth remains positive, margin compression, rising SG&A costs, and elevated inventory levels suggest emerging operational headwinds.
analyst_note: Lululemon delivered moderate 6.5% revenue growth in Q2 2025, but profitability was pressured by a ~100 bps gross margin contraction and rising operating costs, resulting in a 5.6% YoY decline in net income. The $280M+ inventory build since year-end is a key metric to watch for potential promotional risks in the second half of the year.
